nodes.context: Yakuza gang based in Kobe, Japan; largest affiliate of Yamaguchi-gumi until 2015.
Largest known yakuza syndicate in Japan.
Nagoya-based yakuza group, affiliate of Yamaguchi-gumi.
Splinter group formed by Yamaken-gumi and others after withdrawing from Yamaguchi-gumi in 2015.
Yakuza group; former member Kenichi Yamamoto was a member.
Yakuza group; Yoshinori Watanabe was 1st kaicho.
Yakuza group; Kaneyoshi Kuwata was kaicho.
Yakuza group; Kunio Inoue was kaicho.
Yakuza group; Koji Nakata was kaicho.

edges.evidence_quote: It was the largest affiliate, followed by the Nagoya -based Kodo-kai , of the largest known yakuza syndicate in Japan, the Yamaguchi-gumi until 2015.
edges.context: Yamaken-gumi was the largest affiliate of Yamaguchi-gumi until 2015.

edges.evidence_quote: It was the largest affiliate, followed by the Nagoya -based Kodo-kai , of the largest known yakuza syndicate in Japan, the Yamaguchi-gumi until 2015.
edges.context: Both were affiliates of Yamaguchi-gumi, but no direct relationship between them is stated.

edges.evidence_quote: In 2015, the Yamaken-gumi, along with several other organisations, withdrew from the Yamaguchi-gumi and formed the Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi . From 2015 to 2020, it was under the umbrella of the Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi.
edges.context: Yamaken-gumi was a founding member and later under the umbrella of Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi from 2015 to 2020.

edges.evidence_quote: On 16 September 2021, the Yamaguchi-gumi announced that it will be welcoming back Yamaken-gumi members who had disunited from them in 2015. As a result, the Yamaken-gumi once again became the umbrella organisation of the Yamaguchi-gumi.
edges.context: Yamaken-gumi reunited with Yamaguchi-gumi in 2021, becoming its umbrella organization.

edges.evidence_quote: In 2015, the Yamaken-gumi, along with several other organisations, withdrew from the Yamaguchi-gumi and formed the Kobe Yamaguchi-gumi.
edges.context: Yamaken-gumi withdrew from Yamaguchi-gumi in 2015, indicating a split.

edges.evidence_quote: He was a former member of Yasuhara-kai ( 安原会 ) .
edges.context: Founder Kenichi Yamamoto was a former member of Yasuhara-kai.

edges.evidence_quote: He was 1st kaicho ( 会長 ) of the Kenryu-kai ( 健竜会 )
edges.context: Second kumicho Yoshinori Watanabe was 1st kaicho of Kenryu-kai.

edges.evidence_quote: He was kaicho of the Second Kenryu-kai .
edges.context: Third kumicho Kaneyoshi Kuwata was kaicho of Second Kenryu-kai.

edges.evidence_quote: He was kaicho of the Fourth Kenryu-kai .
edges.context: Fourth kumicho Kunio Inoue was kaicho of Fourth Kenryu-kai.

edges.evidence_quote: He was kaicho of the Fifth Kenryu-kai .
edges.context: Fifth kumicho Koji Nakata was kaicho of Fifth Kenryu-kai.
